bibliographic_data.title: UD-KSL Treebank v1.3: A semi-automated framework for aligning XPOS-extracted units with UPOS tags
bibliographic_data.abstract: The present study extends recent work on Universal Dependencies annotations for second-language (L2) Korean by introducing a semi-automated framework that identifies morphosyntactic constructions from XPOS sequences and aligns those constructions with corresponding UPOS categories. We also broaden the existing L2-Korean corpus by annotating 2,998 new sentences from argumentative essays. To evaluate the impact of XPOS-UPOS alignments, we fine-tune L2-Korean morphosyntactic analysis models on datasets both with and without these alignments, using two NLP toolkits. Our results indicate that the aligned dataset not only improves consistency across annotation layers but also enhances morphosyntactic tagging and dependency-parsing accuracy, particularly in cases of limited annotated data.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study introduces a semi-automated framework for aligning XPOS tags with UPOS categories to improve consistency and accuracy in L2-Korean morphosyntactic analysis.
core_content.key_methodology: A semi-automated framework that combines automation with targeted human validation, using the structure of Korean eojeols (morphosyntactic units) to align XPOS tags with UPOS categories. The study also annotates 2,998 new sentences from argumentative essays.
outcomes: Enhanced morphosyntactic tagging and dependency-parsing accuracy
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper introduces a semi-automated framework for aligning XPOS tags with UPOS categories in L2-Korean UD annotations. This framework is designed to improve the consistency of annotation layers, particularly in cases where manual validation is limited. The study also expands the existing L2-Korean corpus by annotating 2,998 new sentences from argumentative essays. By fine-tuning morphosyntactic analysis models on datasets with and without XPOS-UPOS alignments using two NLP toolkits, the researchers demonstrate that alignment improves tagging and dependency-parsing accuracy, especially in low-resource settings.
